properties.name: RAPID 26.5°N
properties.description: Flagship AMOC monitoring array across 26.5°N. Overturning transport, Gulf Stream, Ekman and heat transport, Apr 2004–2024 (annual releases).

properties.name: Florida Current cable
properties.description: Submarine-cable transport of the Florida Current since 1982 — the near-real-time pulse of the AMOC's western boundary.

properties.name: MOVE 16°N
properties.description: Deep-limb (NADW) transport array at 16°N, 2000–present — the longest continuous deep AMOC record.

properties.name: OSNAP West (Labrador Sea)
properties.description: Subpolar overturning array, Labrador Sea section, 2014–2022. Where AMOC weakening and deep-convection signals emerge.

properties.name: OSNAP East (Iceland–Scotland)
properties.description: Subpolar overturning array, eastern section from Greenland to Scotland, 2014–2022.

properties.name: SAMBA 34.5°S
properties.description: South Atlantic MOC basin-wide array at 34.5°S — the AMOC's southern boundary constraint (and the latitude of the FovS early-warning indicator).

properties.name: Denmark Strait overflow
properties.description: Dense overflow water monitoring between Greenland and Iceland — source waters of the AMOC's lower limb (accessible via AMOCatlas).

properties.name: North Atlantic 'cold blob'
properties.description: Subpolar-gyre region whose relative cooling is the classic SST fingerprint of AMOC slowdown (Caesar et al. 2018; Ditlevsen & Ditlevsen 2023).

properties.name: Mauna Loa Observatory
properties.description: Home of the Keeling curve — continuous atmospheric CO2 since 1958 (NOAA GML + Scripps).

properties.name: South Pole Observatory
properties.description: NOAA GML baseline observatory — CO2 record since 1957, the Southern Hemisphere anchor.

properties.name: Cape Grim
properties.description: Baseline air pollution station, Tasmania — key AGAGE site for CO2, CH4, N2O and halocarbons since 1976.

properties.name: Zeppelin (Ny-Ålesund)
properties.description: Arctic GAW station on Svalbard — background GHG and aerosol monitoring at 79°N.

properties.name: Jungfraujoch
properties.description: High-alpine ICOS/GAW station (3,580 m) in Switzerland — free-troposphere GHG record since 1972.
